ShortDescription.Value: Turn your city's metro network into a clean, readable metro map.

Publish.LongDescription:
Turn the metro/subway network of your Cities: Skylines II city into a clean, official-style metro map.
This mod exports your city's metro network to a JSON file. You then open that file in the free companion Windows Viewer app to render the map and save it as an SVG (great for wallpapers, posters, or sharing your transit system).
How to use: 1. In game: Options > CS2 Metro Diagram, pick an export folder, and click "Export Real Metro JSON". 2. Open the Viewer app, click "Open Default Export", and your map appears. 3. Adjust the layout/size if you like, then save the SVG.
The default map layout draws lines horizontal, vertical, or 45-degree, renders lines that share a track as parallel lines, and fills the canvas — the same conventions real metro maps use.
Download the Viewer and full source from GitHub: https://github.com/Eriksas/Cities-Skylines-2-Metro-Viewer
Alpha notes: - Metro/subway only. - Exports data only; it never edits your city. - No in-game preview yet — rendering happens in the Viewer app.
Publish.ChangeLog: Alpha.2: - New default map: schematic-anneal, a clean octilinear layout by global optimization. Shared tracks now render as parallel lines and the map fills the canvas. - Straight lines stay straight (fixed kinks on through-running lines). - Redesigned Viewer: new look, opens your city fit-to-width right away, advanced settings tucked away. - Simplified in-game options: pick an export folder and click Export Real Metro JSON. Developer test/debug export buttons removed. - Geographic mode is still available for faithful real geometry.
